topStories.title: Quote of the day by John Templeton: "Diversification is a safety factor that is essential because we should be humble enough to admit we can be wrong"
topStories.summary: Legendary investor John Templeton stressed diversification as a key safety factor.  It means spreading investments across various assets. This approach helps manage risk and reduces losses from unexpected market shifts.  During uncertain times, diversified portfolios offer more stability.  Humility and avoiding overconfidence are crucial.  This strategy helps investors preserve capital and navigate market changes with confidence.

The $1.8 billion pivot: How Michael Bloomberg’s historic gift rewrote the rules of college access
india.summary: Michael Bloomberg's $1.8 billion donation to Johns Hopkins University in 2018 revolutionised higher education by funding need-blind admissions. This historic gift eliminated financial barriers for talented students, allowing them to pursue education based on merit, not wealth. The endowment ensures a lasting legacy of opportunity, empowering future generations to excel and contribute to society without the burden of overwhelming debt.

india.title: The $22 million mystery: Why a secret donor handed a small Canadian hospital the gift of a lifetime
india.summary: A remarkable $22 million donation to Yarmouth Hospital Foundation in Nova Scotia has transformed local healthcare. The anonymous donor's gift, intended for perpetual funding through investments, ensures over $1 million annually for patient benefits. This unprecedented act of generosity shifts focus from immediate needs to long-term planning, offering a powerful example of quiet philanthropy's lasting impact on regional communities.

business.title: India underperformed Korea by 180 percentage points; but the worst FII selling may be over, says Vikash Kumar Jain
business.summary: India's stock market has lagged behind other Asian markets. This underperformance has made Indian stocks less expensive. Investor expectations are now lower. The market is positioned for potential gains if global risks decrease. Valuations are no longer stretched. The risk and reward for investors are now more balanced than in recent years.

business.title: Bharti Airtel Q4 Results: Profit falls 34% YoY to Rs 7,325 crore even as revenue jumps 16%
business.summary: Bharti Airtel Q4 Results:  Bharti Airtel reported a consolidated net profit of Rs 7,325 crore for Q4 FY26, a 34% year-on-year decline, despite a 16% revenue growth to Rs 55,383 crore. The company recommended a final dividend of Rs 24 per share and highlighted strong performance in India and Africa, alongside strategic investments in digital lending and AI.

business.title: PMS Tracker: Money Grow Asset, Green Portfolio, 12 others post up to 44% returns in April
business.summary: A range of quantitative, thematic, multi-cap and small-cap PMS strategies posted strong gains in April, with top performers delivering returns of up to 44.39% amid a sharp equity market rebound. Broader markets led the rally, while debt-focused strategies largely underperformed or delivered muted gains.

business.title: Adani Power seeks Rs 8,000 crore in debt to fund expansion
business.summary: The fundraising includes 50 billion rupees of public debt and around 30 billion rupees of loans from a group of lenders led by State Bank of India, the people said, asking not to be identified as the information is private.

business.title: Tata Motors (CV) Q4 Results: Standalone profit spikes 70% YoY to Rs 2,406 crore; revenue jumps 22%
business.summary: Tata Motors CV Q4 Results:  Tata Motors (CV) achieved a strong financial performance. Net profit surged 70% year-on-year to Rs 2,406 crore in the March quarter. Revenue also saw a healthy 22% increase, reaching Rs 24,452 crore. The company's board recommended a final dividend of Rs 4 per equity share. Full-year revenue stood at Rs 77,400 crore.

business.title: A short squeeze or sentiment rally? Here's why SAIL shares surged 14% today
business.summary: Shares of Steel Authority of India (SAIL) jumped 14% on Wednesday, driven largely by a short squeeze as traders with heavy bearish derivative positions were forced to cover shorts. Extremely high MWPL utilisation and concentrated bets among a few clients amplified the rally as stop-loss triggers accelerated buying pressure.

business.title: Blackstone-backed Knowledge Realty Trust raises Rs 500 cr through issue of commercial papers
business.summary: Knowledge Realty Trust (KRT), India's largest REIT, has successfully raised Rs 500 crore through commercial paper issuance. This private placement will fund KRT's expansion plans. The 91-day commercial papers, set to mature on August 11, 2026, will be listed on the BSE's wholesale debt market.

business.title: Indian rupee hits record low as outflows, oil strain worsen rout
business.summary: The energy shock from the prolonged U.S.-Iran war ‌that has effectively ⁠shut ⁠the Strait of Hormuz has hurt India's macroeconomic outlook and strained its current account balance. Economists have ​lowered their growth forecasts, raised inflation projections and warned of sustained pressure on the rupee.

business.title: Sri Lotus Developers and Realty Q4 profit rises 11% to Rs 96 cr, revenue grows to Rs 322 cr
business.summary: Sri Lotus Developers and Realty Ltd reported an 11% increase in its consolidated net profit to Rs 95.57 crore for the quarter ended March. Total income for the fourth quarter surged to Rs 322.03 crore. For the full fiscal year 2025-26, the company's net profit grew to Rs 237.08 crore, with total income rising to Rs 818.71 crore.

business.title: Gold import duty hike in US may have led to insider trading, says Zerodha’s Nithin Kamath
business.summary: Zerodha CEO Nithin Kamath highlighted the tight regulation in Indian financial markets following a surprise import duty hike on gold and silver. He noted the absence of unusual trading activity before the announcement, contrasting it with potential scenarios in Western markets where privileged information might be exploited.
